title: Choppin’ It Up: TGI Friday CEO’s Plans for Top-Line Growth
summary: TGI Friday’s is eliminating busy work from their general manager’s plates, allowing them to focus on the team members, Sugarloaf Holdings Founder and CEO Ray Blanchette tells Bloomberg Intelligence. In this episode of the Choppin’ It Up podcast, Blanchette sits down with BI’s senior restaurant and foodservice analyst Michael Halen to discuss TGI Friday’s focus on four-wall execution, hospitality and food quality to boost US same-store sales. title: Vanguards of Healthcare: SR One’s George on Next Biotech Cycle
summary: “I think this is an incredible time to be investing in biotech,” says Dr. Simeon George, founder and CEO of SR One, who joins Bloomberg Intelligence analyst Sam Fazeli to discuss why biotech’s next cycle will be global, selective and unforgiving. George traces SR One’s evolution from one of pharma’s oldest corporate venture arms to an independent firm with more than $2 billion raised. title: Mexico’s Energy Future Sits Between Two Superpowers (Podcast)
summary: Mexico is entering a pivotal period for its energy system. A new wave of reforms is opening the door to greater private investment in clean power, storage and grid infrastructure after years of subdued activity, creating one of the region’s largest energy transition opportunities. The shift comes against a complex geopolitical backdrop. title: What Nigeria Stocks Tell Investors About Africa Markets: Live Q&A
summary: Bloomberg reporters answer your questions about Nigeria’s world-beating stocks, and where investors are finding opportunities in African markets, in a Live Q&A on Friday, July 17 at 12 p.m. GMT

title: Chip Stocks Hit by AI Spending Worry as Oil Climbs: Markets Wrap
summary: A selloff in chipmakers weighed on stocks amid concerns over whether massive artificial-intelligence investments will justify lofty valuations while higher oil prices lifted bond yields.

title: Gold Falls as US-Iran Hostilities Rekindles Fed Hike Bets
summary: Gold declined as escalating tensions in the Middle East rekindled expectations that the Federal Reserve may need to hike interest rates to contain stubborn inflation.

title: Emerging Currencies Dip as Oil, Dollar Spike Over Iran Worries
summary: Most developing-world currencies fell Thursday as renewed worries about tensions in the Middle East lifted oil prices in another risk-off session in global markets.

title: Faena Condo Project Sells $30 Million Penthouse on Miami River
summary: Argentine fashion entrepreneur-turned-developer Alan Faena sold a $30 million penthouse in his planned twin condo towers on the Miami River, a milestone in the mission to take his maximalist, theatrical brand worldwide.

title: Jump Trading Doubles Team to Ride Record Prediction Market Boom
summary: As prediction markets draw record trading around events like the World Cup, Jump Trading Group is betting the once-niche contracts are becoming a lasting corner of Wall Street.

title: Citigroup Sees Bondholders Recover 50% of Senegal’s Debt Under Revamp
summary: Holders of Senegal’s dollar bonds could end up recouping less than 50% of the face value of the securities in the event of a restructuring — below the prices at which the debt currently trades, according to Citigroup Inc.

title: Canada’s Forgotten Shale Gas Play Reemerges as Oil Hot Spot
summary: On the edge of the Canadian Rocky Mountains, companies including Obsidian Energy Ltd. and Yangarra Resources Corp. are breathing new life into a long-dormant gas reservoir — except now they’re drilling for oil.

title: German Chemical Industry in Crisis Despite Reprieve, Lobby Says
summary: Germany’s chemical industry remains in a structural crisis despite a brief breather when the conflict in the Middle East triggered stockpiling and hit rivals in Asia harder, according to the industry’s VCI association.

title: Abbott Laboratories Surges on 2026 Profit Guidance Raise
summary: Abbott Laboratories soared the most in 24 years after raising its 2026 profit guidance following a stronger-than-expected second quarter driven by improved performance across most of its business lines.

title: Raiffeisen Clears Key Hurdle in €517 Million Bid for Addiko Bank
summary: Raiffeisen Bank International AG reached a key milestone in its bid to acquire Addiko Bank AG after shareholders representing more than 55% tendered their shares, reaching the offer’s minimum threshold.

title: Gaming Bonds Lose Allure as Threat From Prediction Markets Grows
summary: Investors increasingly see gaming bonds as a losing bet.

title: Hoisington, US Bond Bull for Decades, Turns Decidedly Bearish
summary: Hoisington Investment Management Co., the bond manager known for its bullish stance on US Treasuries going back more than 30 years, has turned bearish.

title: Why IPOs of Tiny Foreign Companies Have Vanished in US
summary: Added scrutiny on foreign microcaps puts a damper on public debuts

title: US Jobless Claims Decline to 208,000, Below Forecasts
summary: Applications for US unemployment benefits fell last week, suggesting the labor market remains stable.

title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 16
summary: Oil resumed gains as concerns mount about disruptions to flows from the Middle East, with the US continuing to strike Iran.

title: Multistrat Hedge Fund Paloma to Cut Teams in Latest Pivot
summary: Donald Sussman, the veteran behind Paloma Partners, is pivoting his long-standing multistrategy hedge fund to focus on fewer managers in which he has the highest conviction as the firm adjusts to a decline in assets over the past three years.

title: SMBC Bolsters Global Sales and Trading Push With Macro Hire
summary: Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corp. hired Kunal Maini as international head of macro rates, bolstering efforts to expand its sales and trading franchise beyond its core Japanese market.

title: US Drivers Are Again Paying More Than $5 a Gallon for Diesel
summary: American drivers are once again paying more than $5 a gallon for diesel, with a fresh round of hostilities in the Middle East raising the specter of more war-induced inflation.

title: US Stock Futures Today: AtaiBeckley, GE Aerospace, UnitedHealth
summary: S&P 500 Index futures are down 0.2% as of 7:47 a.m. in New York. The US intensified strikes against Iran, hitting an oil tanker near the country’s main export terminal for the first time since the restart of the blockade on the Islamic Republic’s ports.
